name: Uusimaa (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Uusimaa arms, evoking the Gulf of Finland and southern coastal waters
the two silver wavy fesses of the arms, evoking the sea and the waters of the region
the golden boat of the arms, referring to the seafaring, coastal character of the region

description: A blue flag bearing a golden boat with a rudder between two silver wavy fesses, the heraldic banner of the Uusimaa coat of arms.
history: Uusimaa became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic arms of the Uusimaa province on Finland's southern coast.

features.role: two silver wavy fesses, one near the top and one near the base, spanning the width of the flag
features.symbolism: the silver wavy bars of the Uusimaa arms, evoking the sea and the waters of the region
the golden boat of the Uusimaa arms, referring to the seafaring, coastal character of Finland's southern shore
